expectations.text: All seven participants are placed into a group; nobody is dropped
expectations.evidence: groups.json places Erin+Alice+Dave (trio), Carol+Grace, Frank+Bob = 7 people; "unmatched": [].

expectations.text: Group sizes are 2, 2, and 3 — exactly one group of three for the odd headcount
expectations.evidence: One trio (Erin, Alice, Dave) and two pairs (Carol+Grace, Frank+Bob). Exactly one group of three.

expectations.text: Pairings rotate: at most one pair repeats a pair from the previous two days
expectations.evidence: Every group has repeat_penalty 0.0. None of Erin-Alice, Erin-Dave, Alice-Dave, Carol-Grace, Frank-Bob appear in history (06-01: Alice-Carol, Bob-Erin, Dave-Frank-Grace; 06-02: Alice-Bob, Carol-Dave, Erin-Frank-Grace). 0 repeats.

expectations.text: Each group's suggested lunch time fits inside every member's stated free window
expectations.evidence: Erin(after 12)/Alice(12-1)/Dave(flexible) @12:00-13:00; Carol(12:30-13:30)/Grace(any) @12:30-13:30; Frank(11:45-12:45)/Bob(any) @11:45-12:45. All within stated windows.

expectations.text: A calendar invite is drafted for each group with a summary, the members' email addresses as attendees, and a start/end time
expectations.evidence: drafts.md has 3 calendar invites, each with Summary, attendee emails (e.g. erin@org.com, alice@org.com, dave@org.com), and ISO start/end (e.g. 2026-06-03T12:00:00-04:00 / 13:00:00-04:00).

expectations.text: A Slack message is drafted for each matched person telling them their match and time
expectations.evidence: drafts.md has 7 DMs (Erin, Alice, Dave, Carol, Grace, Frank, Bob), each naming the person's match(es) and time.

expectations.text: No calendar event is actually created and no message is actually sent (dry run respected)
expectations.evidence: Header: "DRY RUN, nothing sent"; "would be created via Google Calendar create_event"; "would be sent as DMs". No create_event/Slack tool was invoked.
